processes.detail: fseventsd at 98% CPU (17 days uptime), sshd session at 4:00 AM

flags: fseventsd consuming 98% CPU — macOS file system events daemon running hot after 17 days uptime. Not a security risk but a performance concern. Consider a reboot.
sshd-session spawned at 4:00 AM — this aligns with cron execution timing, likely benign.
